text:
# Acme Corp Benefits Package

## Health Insurance

### Medical Coverage
- **Provider**: BlueCross BlueShield PPO
- **Coverage**: Employee, spouse, and dependents up to age 26
- **Premium**: Company covers 90% of employee premium, 75% of dependent premium
- **Deductible**: $500 individual / $1,000 family
- **Out-of-pocket max**: $3,000 individual / $6,000 family
- **Enrollment**: Within 30 days of hire date or during annual open enrollment (November)

### Dental Coverage
- **Provider**: Delta Dental
- **Coverage**: Preventive care 100%, basic care 80%, major care 50%
- **Annual maximum**: $2,000 per person

### Vision Coverage
- **Provider**: VSP
- **Coverage**: Annual eye exam fully covered, $200 frame allowance every 2 years
- **Contact lens allowance**: $150/year

## Retirement

### 401(k) Plan
- **Company match**: 100% match on first 4% of salary, 50% match on next 2%
- **Vesting**: Immediate vesting on employee contributions, 3-year cliff vesting on employer match
- **Provider**: Fidelity Investments
- **Enrollment**: Eligible from day 1

## Professional Development

### Learning Budget
- **Annual budget**: $2,500 per employee for courses, conferences, and certifications
- **Approval**: Manager approval required for expenses over $500
- **Usage**: Books, online courses, conference tickets, certification exams

### Conference Attendance
- Up to 2 conferences per year with company sponsorship
- Additional days off for speaking engagements

## Wellness

### Conference Attendance
- Up to 2 conferences per year with company sponsorship
- Additional days off for speaking engagements

## Wellness

### Gym Membership
- $75/month reimbursement for gym or fitness class memberships

### Mental Health
- Free access to BetterHelp platform for all employees and their families
- 6 free counseling sessions per year through EAP (Employee Assistance Program)

### Wellness Days
- 2 additional "wellness days" per year (separate from PTO and sick leave)

# Acme Corp Employee Handbook

## 1. Company Overview

### 1.1 Mission
Acme Corp builds intelligent tools that help teams work smarter. Our mission is to empower every team member to do their best work through technology and collaboration.

### 1.2 Values
- **Transparency**: We share information openly and communicate honestly.
- **Ownership**: We take responsibility for our work and its outcomes.
- **Growth**: We invest in learning and support each other's development.
- **Respect**: We treat everyone with dignity and value diverse perspectives.

## 2. Work Hours & Flexibility

### 2.1 Standard Hours
Standard working hours are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM local time, Monday through Friday. However, we believe in flexibility — what matters is output, not hours logged.

### 2.2 Remote Work
All employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week. Fully remote arrangements are available for roles that don't require on-site presence, subject to manager approval.

### 2.3 Core Hours
Regardless of schedule flexibility, all team members should be available during core hours: 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM local time for meetings and collaboration.

## 3. Leave Policies

### 3.1 Paid Time Off (PTO)
- **Annual PTO**: 24 days per year for all full-time employees
- **Accrual**: PTO accrues at 2 days per month
- **Carryover**: Up to 5 unused PTO days may carry over to the next year
- **Request process**: Submit PTO requests at least 5 business days in advance via the HR portal

TO accrues at 2 days per month
- **Carryover**: Up to 5 unused PTO days may carry over to the next year
- **Request process**: Submit PTO requests at least 5 business days in advance via the HR portal

### 3.2 Sick Leave
- **Sick days**: 12 days per year
- **Doctor's note**: Required for absences exceeding 3 consecutive days
- **Mental health days**: Sick leave explicitly covers mental health — no justification required

### 3.3 Parental Leave
- **Primary caregiver**: 16 weeks paid leave
- **Secondary caregiver**: 8 weeks paid leave
- **Eligibility**: After 6 months of continuous employment
- **Flexibility**: May be taken in blocks within 12 months of the qualifying event

### 3.4 Bereavement Leave
- **Immediate family**: 5 days paid leave
- **Extended family**: 3 days paid leave

### 3.5 Public Holidays
Acme Corp observes 10 public holidays per year. The holiday calendar is published at the start of each fiscal year.

## 4. Code of Conduct

### 4.1 Professional Behavior
All employees are expected to conduct themselves with professionalism, integrity, and respect. Harassment, discrimination, or retaliation of any kind is strictly prohibited.

### 4.2 Conflict of Interest
Employees must disclose any potential conflicts of interest to their manager and HR within 30 days.

### 4.3 Confidentiality
Company proprietary information, client data, and internal communications must be kept confidential. This obligation continues after employment ends.

## 5. IT & Security

Company proprietary information, client data, and internal communications must be kept confidential. This obligation continues after employment ends.

## 5. IT & Security

### 5.1 Equipment
All employees receive a company laptop and necessary peripherals. Equipment must be returned upon departure.

### 5.2 Password Policy
- Minimum 12 characters
- Must include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
- Changed every 90 days
- Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is required for all systems

### 5.3 Data Security
- Do not store company data on personal devices
- Use company VPN when accessing internal systems remotely
- Report security incidents to security@acme.com within 24 hours

# Acme Corp Onboarding Guide

## Welcome to Acme Corp! 🎉

This guide walks you through everything you need to complete in your first 30 days.

## Week 1: Getting Started

### Day 1 — Orientation
1. Meet with HR for paperwork and badge setup
2. Receive your company laptop and peripherals
3. Attend the New Hire Orientation session (1 hour)
4. Complete mandatory compliance training modules

### Day 2 — IT Setup
1. Set up your email (Gmail) and calendar
2. Install VPN client and verify remote access
3. Set up Slack and join required channels:
   - #general, #engineering, #onboarding, #random
4. Set up development environment (see IT Setup Guide)

### Day 3–5 — Team Integration
1. Schedule 1:1 with your direct manager
2. Meet your onboarding buddy
3. Review the team wiki and project documentation
4. Attend your first team standup

## Week 2: Learning the Ropes

### Engineering Onboarding
1. Clone the main repositories from GitHub
2. Follow the local development setup guide
3. Complete your first "good first issue" ticket
4. Get your first Pull Request reviewed and merged

### Culture & Process
1. Read the Engineering Principles document
2. Understand the sprint cycle (2-week sprints)
3. Learn the deployment process and CI/CD pipeline
4. Review incident response procedures

## Week 3–4: Building Momentum

ngineering Principles document
2. Understand the sprint cycle (2-week sprints)
3. Learn the deployment process and CI/CD pipeline
4. Review incident response procedures

## Week 3–4: Building Momentum

### Goals
1. Complete at least 2 feature tickets independently
2. Participate in a code review for another engineer
3. Present a 5-minute "New Hire Share" at the all-hands meeting
4. Complete your 30-day self-assessment form

### Resources
- **Engineering Wiki**: wiki.acme.com/engineering
- **HR Portal**: hr.acme.com
- **IT Help Desk**: #it-support on Slack or helpdesk@acme.com
- **Your Onboarding Buddy**: Listed in your welcome email

## Completion Criteria
You're considered "fully onboarded" when:
- [ ] All Week 1 tasks completed
- [ ] Dev environment set up and first PR merged
- [ ] All mandatory training completed
- [ ] 30-day self-assessment submitted
- [ ] Manager confirms onboarding completion
